evals.prompt: Plan a sprint from SPEC-0015 for the claude-plugin-design repo. Use GitHub issues. The repo is joestump/claude-plugin-design.
evals.expected_output: Creates an epic issue and 3-4 story issues in GitHub with requirement checklists, branch naming, PR conventions, foundation story detection, hotspot analysis, and a dependency graph.

assertions.text: Epic issue created with 'epic' label and title starting with 'Implement'
3-4 story issues created, each with a ## Requirements section containing task checklists
Each requirement checklist item references SPEC-0015 and includes WHEN/THEN scenarios derived from the spec
Each story issue body has a ### Branch section with feature/{issue-number}-{slug} pattern after issue creation (two-pass)
Each story issue body has a ### PR Convention section with 'Closes #{issue-number}' keyword
Branch slug is kebab-case derived from the story title, max 50 chars
Foundation story detection ran: shared types/packages needed by 2+ stories identified and extracted into foundation-labeled stories
Hotspot analysis ran: recent git history analyzed and hotspot files reported with percentages
Dependency graph output showing foundation stories, serialized stories (if hotspots), and parallelizable stories
Every ### Requirement section from the spec appears in exactly one story's task checklist
GitHub Project V2 created and linked to the repository
Report suggests /design:prime before starting implementation

evals.prompt: Break down SPEC-0016 into GitHub issues for joestump/claude-plugin-design. Use --no-branches and --no-projects flags.
evals.expected_output: Creates epic and story issues without ### Branch, ### PR Convention sections, and without creating a GitHub Project.

assertions.text: Epic issue created with 'epic' label
3-4 story issues created with ## Requirements sections
No ### Branch section appears in any issue body
No ### PR Convention section appears in any issue body
No GitHub Project created (report says 'skipped')
Report confirms branch naming was skipped and PR conventions were skipped
Stories that involve HTTP endpoints include a ## Security Checklist section with authentication, input validation, output encoding, rate limiting, and body size limits
Security checklist is NOT added to stories that don't involve HTTP endpoints
CI foundation story created with static analysis, race detection, and formatting checks (language-agnostic terms)
CI story uses 'ci' and 'foundation' labels

evals.prompt: Plan a sprint from SPEC-0012 with --scrum flag. Use GitHub issues for joestump/claude-plugin-design.
evals.expected_output: Runs full scrum ceremony: spec completeness audit, issue decomposition, 5-agent grooming team, dissent resolution, automatic organize, automatic enrich, and sprint report.

assertions.text: User informed that scrum ceremony is starting with expected phases
Spec completeness audit ran: checked for design.md pairing and reported pass/missing counts
Five specialist agents spawned: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Engineer A, Engineer B (Grumpy), Architect
Each agent reviewed every story and submitted a verdict (APPROVED, REVISE, or DEFER)
Dissent resolution ran for any stories with non-APPROVED verdicts
Organize step ran automatically after grooming (no user prompt required)
Enrich step ran automatically after organize (no user prompt required)
Sprint report output contains: Spec Completeness, Grooming Results (Accepted/Revised/Deferred), Final Sprint Backlog, and Next Steps sections
Sprint report includes story sizes (XS/S/M/L/XL) assigned by Scrum Master
Sprint report recommends /design:work and /design:prime as next steps

evals.prompt: Plan a sprint from SPEC-9999 for joestump/claude-plugin-design using GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: Reports that the spec cannot be found and suggests creating one with /design:spec.

assertions.text: Error message indicates SPEC-9999 was not found or does not exist
Suggests /design:spec to create a new specification
No issues created in the tracker
No GitHub Project created

evals.prompt: Work on SPEC-0015 issues for joestump/claude-plugin-design. Use --max-agents 2.
evals.expected_output: Discovers open issues for SPEC-0015, creates worktrees, dispatches up to 2 parallel workers with sibling PR manifest, implements issues, creates PRs with lifecycle label transitions.

assertions.text: Parallelism limit resolved to 2 from --max-agents flag
Report shows 'Starting N of M ready stories (Q queued, max-parallel-agents: 2)'
Lifecycle labels (queued, in-progress, in-review, merged) created or verified to exist
All workable issues initially labeled 'queued'
Issues transitioned from 'queued' to 'in-progress' when assigned to a worker (previous label removed first)
Issues transitioned from 'in-progress' to 'in-review' when PR created (previous label removed first)
Sibling PR manifest built before dispatching workers, containing file ownership and shared types sections
Each worker received spec.md, design.md, ADR content, and sibling PR manifest in their context
Workers broadcast FILE_CLAIM via SendMessage before modifying files
No more than 2 agents active concurrently; excess stories queued
Queue status reported after each agent completion: 'Active: N/2, Queued: Q, Completed: C, Failed: F, Blocked: B'
Final report includes Results table with Issue, Branch, PR, and Status columns

evals.expected_output: Outputs a preview table of what would happen without creating worktrees, branches, or PRs.

assertions.text: Output contains '## Dry Run' heading
Preview table includes columns: #, Issue, Branch, Status
Blocked issues shown with dependency reason (e.g., 'Blocked (depends on #XX)')
Skipped issues listed with reasons (epic, no ### Branch section)
Output ends with 'No changes were made.'
No git worktrees created
No branches created
No PRs created
No lifecycle labels applied to issues

evals.prompt: Implement issues 42 43 44 for joestump/claude-plugin-design. The CLAUDE.md has max-parallel-agents set to 3.
evals.expected_output: Picks up the 3 specific issues, creates worktrees, dispatches workers respecting the CLAUDE.md parallelism config, validates protected paths, and creates PRs.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md read for ## Design Plugin Configuration section to resolve max-parallel-agents
Worktrees created at .claude/worktrees/{branch-name} for each issue
Workers validate diff against protected paths (spec-dir, adr-dir, CLAUDE.md, .claude-plugin-design.json) before staging
If any protected file was modified, it is reverted via git checkout and recorded in ### Deferred Design Doc Updates PR body section
Governing comments added in source code files (not subject to design doc isolation)
Each PR body includes close keywords referencing the issue number
Topological merge order computed after all PRs reach in-review, showing tier classification
Merge order table shown to user with Order, PR, Issue, Overlapping Files, and Action columns
User asked to approve merge order before merging begins

evals.prompt: Run /design:work with no arguments for joestump/claude-plugin-design.
evals.expected_output: Reviews the full backlog, proposes a batch of issues biased toward unblocking work, and asks the user to approve before starting.

assertions.text: Backlog scanned: all open non-epic issues fetched from the tracker
Issues without ### Branch sections skipped with suggestion to run /design:enrich
Proposed batch presented via AskUserQuestion with a table showing Issue, Project/Epic, and Rationale columns
Batch proposal biases toward issues that unblock other issues
User given options: 'Approve this batch' or 'Let me pick manually'
No work started until user approves the batch
Dependency ordering respected: blocked issues placed in deferred queue

evals.prompt: Review PRs for SPEC-0009 in joestump/claude-plugin-design. Use 2 reviewer-responder pairs.
evals.expected_output: Discovers open PRs for SPEC-0009, runs conflict-marker CI gate, creates reviewer-responder pairs, reviews against spec acceptance criteria, and merges approved PRs.

assertions.text: Conflict-marker CI gate ran on ALL target PR diffs before any review logic
PR diffs scanned for <<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>> markers
spec.md and design.md loaded as architecture context for reviewers
Team created with 2 reviewer agents and 2 responder agents
PRs distributed to pairs using round-robin assignment
Reviewers checked CI status before reviewing each PR diff
Reviews reference specific spec acceptance criteria or ADR decisions, not just style
Approved PRs merged using squash strategy (default)
Parent epic closure checked after each successful merge
Final report contains Results table with PR, Title, CI, Review, Merge, and Status columns
Final report includes Worktrees section showing reused/created/cleaned counts

evals.expected_output: Outputs a preview table showing which PRs would be reviewed and by which pair, without submitting any reviews or merging.

assertions.text: Output contains '## Dry Run' heading
Preview table includes columns: #, PR, Title, Branch, CI, Pair
Each PR assigned to a pair via round-robin
Output ends with 'No reviews submitted. No merges performed.'
No review comments submitted to any PR
No PRs merged
No team created (dry-run exits before team formation)

evals.prompt: Review PRs for SPEC-0015 in joestump/claude-plugin-design with --no-merge. Assume PR #201 has conflict markers in its diff.
evals.expected_output: PR #201 rejected immediately by the conflict-marker CI gate with REQUEST_CHANGES. Other PRs reviewed normally but not merged due to --no-merge.

assertions.text: Conflict-marker gate ran before any review logic for all PRs
PR #201 received REQUEST_CHANGES review with '## Conflict Markers Detected' heading
Rejection message for #201 includes a table of offending file paths and line numbers
PR #201 skipped entirely for architecture context loading and spec compliance review
PR #201 reported as 'Rejected: conflict markers' in the final summary
Other PRs reviewed normally against spec acceptance criteria
No PRs merged (--no-merge flag respected)
Approved PRs reported as 'approved, pending manual merge'

evals.prompt: Run a full project audit on joestump/claude-plugin-design with no scope filter.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive audit across all six drift categories with structured report, severity assignments, and recommended actions.

assertions.text: All six drift categories analyzed: Code vs. Spec, Code vs. ADR, ADR vs. Spec, Coverage Gaps, Stale Artifacts, Policy Violations
Report header shows scope as 'Full project' with counts of ADRs, specs, and source files analyzed
Spec pairing validation ran: each spec directory checked for both spec.md and design.md
Unpaired spec artifacts reported as [WARNING] under Stale Artifacts
MUST/SHALL violations assigned [CRITICAL] severity
SHOULD/RECOMMENDED violations assigned [WARNING] severity
Coverage gaps assigned [INFO] severity
Summary matrix table includes all six categories with Critical, Warning, Info, and Total columns
Recommended Actions list ordered by severity: critical first, then warning, then info
Findings within each category ordered by severity (critical first)
Full artifact identifiers used (ADR-XXXX, SPEC-XXXX), not abbreviated
File paths include line numbers where possible (e.g., src/path/file.ts:45)

evals.prompt: Audit the architecture of joestump/claude-plugin-design with --scrum flag. Focus on the security topic.
evals.expected_output: Runs standard audit analysis scoped to security, then enters scrum triage ceremony with 5-agent team for prioritization and dispute resolution.

assertions.text: Standard audit analysis completed first (steps 4-6) before triage ceremony begins
Audit scoped to security-related artifacts and code
Five triage agents spawned: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Engineer A, Engineer B (Grumpy), Architect
Findings grouped by functional theme, not by drift category
Engineer B provides substantive challenges to findings, not generic approvals
ADRs (accepted) and specs (approved/implemented) treated as source of truth
Triage report produced with remediation priorities
--review mode NOT activated (--scrum takes precedence)

evals.prompt: Run /design:audit on an empty project directory that has no docs/adrs/ or docs/openspec/specs/ directories.
evals.expected_output: Reports that no design artifacts were found and suggests creating ADRs or specs.

assertions.text: Message indicates docs/adrs/ directory does not exist, suggests /design:adr
Message indicates docs/openspec/specs/ directory does not exist, suggests /design:spec
Reports 'No design artifacts found' when neither ADRs nor specs exist
No audit report table produced (nothing to analyze)
Does not error or crash on missing directories

evals.prompt: Review PRs for SPEC-0003 in joestump/claude-plugin-design. No open PRs exist for this spec.
evals.expected_output: Informs the user that no open PRs were found and suggests running /design:work to create them.

assertions.text: Message indicates no open PRs were found matching SPEC-0003
Suggests running /design:work to create PRs from planned issues
No team created (nothing to review)
No reviews submitted
